'''Jamie Rooney''' (born {{birth date|df=y|1980|3|17}}) is an English former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who is currently the head coach of his hometown amateur club [[Featherstone Lions]]. He is also a former England international, being an integral part of the 2006 [[Federation Shield]] winning side.<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

He played for [[Featherstone Rovers]] in 1998 in the [[Treize Tournoi]] against Limoux.<ref>{{cite web|title=Rovers ace Rooney notches his 1,000th point|url=http://www.pontefractandcastlefordexpress.co.uk/sport/more-local-sport/rovers-ace-rooney-notches-his-1-000th-point-1-1224154|website=Pontefract and Castleford Express|accessdate=8 June 2015|date=8 April 2002}}</ref> He made his league début in 1999 away at [[Whitehaven R.L.F.C.|Whitehaven]], coming off the substitutes bench and scoring one goal, and quickly became the first choice no. 7 at age 19. In two consecutive seasons he scored more than 100 points in the first 10 games, he is also in the Featherstone Rovers record books for most points in a season. He joined [[Castleford Tigers]] on trial in 2001, but was not offered a permanent contract.<ref>{{cite web |title=Jamie Rooney inspires Barrow to Challenge Cup upset at Castleford |url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2010/apr/18/jamie-rooney-barrow-castleford-challenge-cup?CMP=gu_com |website=The Guardian |access-date=5 June 2022 |date=18 April 2010}}</ref> He joined [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] in December 2002.<ref>{{cite web|title=Rugby league: Wildcats sign Rooney|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/sport/3039004/Rugby-league-Wildcats-sign-Rooney.html|website=Telegraph|accessdate=7 June 2015|date=3 December 2002}}</ref>

The [[England national rugby league team|England]] international had an excellent season with Trinity in 2006, amassing 197 points in 20 games after missing the early part of the season recovering from surgery on a troublesome knee. He shone for Wakefield Trinity again in 2007, playing 25 games and scoring 208 points during 25 appearances. This eventually led to his earning a place in the [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] initial train-on squad for the Gillette Fusion Test series against [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]]. {{Citation needed|date=November 2012}}

Rooney's autobiography, ''High Ambitions'', was released at the end of March 2009.<ref>{{cite book
|title= High Ambitions|last=Rooney|first=Jamie|year=2009|publisher=Vertical Editions|location=UK|isbn= 9781904091295|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=7ipRPgAACAAJ|accessdate=19 February 2011}}</ref>

In July 2009, he was signed on loan to Co-operative Championship title hopefuls [[Barrow Raiders|Barrow]]. After his contract expired at Wakefield, he rejected numerous offers from Super League clubs, instead deciding to stay at Barrow. He was made captain for the 2010 season. He left the club at the end of the 2011 season to join French side [[Limoux Grizzlies|Limoux]] for the 2011-12 [[Elite One Championship]] season. He signed for [[South Wales Ironmen|South Wales Scorpions]] for the 2012 season. South Wales Scorpions released Rooney "by mutual consent" on 11 April 2012, according to the scorpions website. {{Citation needed|date=February 2012}} Rooney went on to play for Whitehaven and Gateshead until July 2014 when he re-signed for Featherstone Rovers.
